findings.description: In hooks/generate-overview.sh and research-ops/scripts/run-cycle.sh, uv run resolves the Python environment from the current working directory. Since these are called from sibling repos, the helper runs under different repo environments, contradicting the goal of a single shared dispatch path with predictable behavior.
findings.fix: Pin uv run to the source project using --project "$SKILLS_ROOT" or equivalent absolute path referencing the shared environment.
findings.title: Shared dispatch environment instability via unpinned uv run

findings.description: The throttling logic uses negative array indices (pids[-$MAX_CONCURRENT]), which is a syntax error in Bash 3.2 (macOS default). Additionally, the script performs a double-wait on child PIDs; once a child is reaped, a subsequent wait returns nonzero, causing successful jobs to be misreported as failures.
findings.fix: Replace negative index logic with a portable worker queue/map and ensure each PID is waited upon exactly once.
findings.title: Unsound and non-portable concurrency logic in generate-overview.sh

findings.description: The regex in pretool-llmx-guard.sh only detects bare 'llmx' tokens. Commands using absolute paths, relative paths, or home-directory shortcuts (e.g., /usr/bin/llmx, ./llmx) bypass the guard entirely, allowing prohibited chat-style automation to proceed.
findings.fix: Harden the parser to be executable-aware, accounting for path prefixes and shell tokenization rather than simple whitespace-delimited regex.
findings.title: Guard bypass for path-qualified llmx invocations

findings.description: The script prints diagnostics on dispatch failure but exits with code 0. This 'fails open' behavior prevents automated supervisors from detecting that the rate-limit fallback failed, treating errors as successful no-ops.
findings.fix: Ensure the script returns a nonzero exit status when the fallback dispatch execution fails.
findings.title: Silent failure in run-cycle.sh automation

findings.description: generate-overview.sh maintains its own OVERVIEW_MODEL to profile mapping and token limits. This duplicates logic already present in shared/llm_dispatch.py, creating a maintenance hazard where profiles can desynchronize between the shell wrappers and the core dispatch system.
findings.fix: Expose profile resolution and token budget metadata via the llm-dispatch.py CLI or a shared metadata file so the shell script can query the source of truth.
findings.title: Duplicated profile and model mapping in shell logic

findings.description: model-review.py imports private internal functions (_add_additional_properties, _strip_additional_properties) from dispatch_core. Internal refactoring of the shared dispatch module will break the review script even if public contracts remain stable.
findings.fix: Refactor model-review.py to use public API methods or move the required logic into a shared public utility module.
findings.title: Fragile coupling to private shared helpers in model-review.py
